title: Ken Goldberg: Good Old-Fashioned Engineering Can Close the 100,000 Year 'Data Gap' in Robotics
snippet: Ken Goldberg is a popular public speaker on AI and robotics, an award-winning roboticist, filmmaker, and artist, and the William S. Floyd Distinguished Chair in Engineering at UC Berkeley. In this talk, he discusses the challenges of robot grasping, the population shifts driving the need for physical intelligence, and why physical AI and robots won't steal human jobs anytime soon.
match_reason: social (video) — This is a recorded keynote presentation delivered by Ken Goldberg himself, focusing on AI, robotics, and physical intelligence.

title: Optimization Is Not All You Need
snippet: In 2019, OpenAI released two million GPT-2 outputs—ungrammatical, half broken—to aid the detection of machine-generated text. The alignment that produced their more fluent successors is usually regarded as an engineering achievement; we read it instead as the newest expression of optimization culture...
match_reason: websearch (publication) — Rita Raley (Professor of English, UC Santa Barbara) is the co-author of this newly published paper on arXiv exploring the cultural logic of optimization in language models.

title: California Launches First-in-the-Nation AI Literacy Initiative with SDSU Leadership
snippet: San Diego State University, in partnership with the California Labor & Workforce Development Agency, California Community Colleges, AWS, and Instructure, launched 'AI-Ready California.' This first-of-its-kind statewide micro-credential program focuses on AI civics, responsibility, and career readiness.
match_reason: websearch (press_release) — This initiative represents a major California public higher education partnership (CSU, SDSU, and California Community Colleges) establishing a statewide standard for AI literacy and workforce training.

title: Assembly Committee Adopts Resolution Urging Intersegmental Dialogue on AI in Higher Education
snippet: The Assembly Higher Education Committee adopted Senate Concurrent Resolution 82 (SCR 82). The measure encourages the leaders of the UC, CSU, and California Community Colleges to convene a joint workgroup to establish unified strategies and best practices for AI use, academic integrity, and student privacy.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — This legislative development directly impacts the University of California system by urging the UC President to collaborate on a statewide public higher education AI policy workgroup.

title: FiRe 2026: AI Meets the University
snippet: In this panel discussion from the Future in Review (FiRe) 2026 conference, UC San Diego Chancellor Pradeep Khosla discusses the complex opportunities AI presents to universities, researchers, and students, pushing back against the idea that AI will make higher education obsolete.
match_reason: social (video) — Pradeep Khosla is the featured speaker in this video, discussing AI's impact on higher education.

title: CITRIS researcher redefines robotics
snippet: A recent feature in Berkeley Engineer magazine highlights the career of CITRIS investigator Ken Goldberg, whose more than three decades of work have helped shape modern robotics... Goldberg believes that the next leap in robotics will come from a combination of 'good old-fashioned engineering' and advances in AI.
match_reason: websearch (blog_post) — This article profiles Ken Goldberg's career, his AUTOLab research, and his views on combining traditional engineering with modern AI.

title: Ken Goldberg on RoboVista Benchmark for Evaluating Robotic VLMs
snippet: Existing VLMs can be very useful for robotics but they struggle to answer multiple-choice questions about robot scenes. This paper explores this gap and provides benchmarks for improvement. Kudos to the team of authors led by postdocs Shuangyu Xie and Eric Chen!
match_reason: social (social_post) — This post was authored directly by Ken Goldberg under his official X handle @Ken_Goldberg, discussing a new robotics benchmark paper he co-authored.

title: The Robot Future - Ken Goldberg Keynote
snippet: Ken Goldberg discusses the intersection of artificial intelligence and physical intelligence (robotics), addressing concerns about physical AI and robots taking human jobs, and highlighting the ongoing global labor shortages.
match_reason: social (video) — This is a recorded keynote presentation delivered by Ken Goldberg, uploaded by his speakers bureau.

title: New CSU Initiatives Invest in Faculty Research, Teaching and Collaboration
snippet: The CSU Chancellor's Office announced funding updates for its systemwide Artificial Intelligence Educational Innovations Challenge. The initiative funds faculty-led projects designed to enable transformative teaching methods, foster research, and address concerns about AI in academics.
match_reason: websearch (press_release) — This announcement details systemwide AI teaching and research grants across the California State University (CSU) system.

title: SCR 82 Assembly Committee on Privacy and Consumer Protection Hearing
snippet: Senate Concurrent Resolution 82 encourages the University of California, California State University, and California Community Colleges to convene a joint workgroup. The group will discuss systemwide strategies for academic integrity, plagiarism mitigation, and student privacy in AI deployment.
match_reason: websearch (position_statement) — This California legislative resolution directly targets the University of California (UC) system's AI policy, governance, and intersegmental collaboration.
